description: Salesforce Certified MuleSoft Developer I mock exam. Platform fundamentals, API design, flows, DataWeave, connectors, basic error handling and deployment. Managed via Salesforce Webassessor/Trailhead since May 2024. Exam conditions: no feedback until the end.

questions.q: Which Anypoint component is used to discover and reuse API specifications published by other teams?
questions.explanation: Exchange is the org-wide catalog for specs, connectors, templates and fragments.

questions.q: An on-prem Mule runtime is managed by Runtime Manager. Which direction is the management connection established?
options: Inbound from MuleSoft to the server
Outbound from the runtime to the control plane
questions.explanation: The agent initiates an outbound TLS connection; no inbound management ports are opened.

questions.q: What are the three top-level parts of a Mule 4 event?
questions.explanation: Event = Message (payload + attributes) + variables. inboundProperties/sessionVars are Mule 3.

questions.q: After an HTTP Request operation succeeds, what happens to the attributes set by the original HTTP Listener?
options: Replaced by the HTTP response attributes
questions.explanation: Every operation replaces both payload and attributes with its result.

questions.q: Which mechanism preserves the current payload while storing an HTTP Request's response elsewhere?
options: The target attribute on the operation
questions.explanation: target/targetValue stores the result in a variable, leaving payload intact.

questions.q: In RAML 1.0, what does 'middleName?: string' declare?
questions.explanation: The ? suffix on a property name marks it optional; RAML properties are required by default.

questions.q: Which RAML construct adds reusable query parameters like page/size to multiple methods?
questions.explanation: Traits are method-level reusable definitions applied with 'is:'.

questions.q: What does the Design Center mocking service use to generate responses?
options: Examples and types declared in the spec
questions.explanation: Mocks derive from the contract: declared examples first, types as fallback.

questions.q: In an APIkit project, a request to a declared resource returns APIKIT:BAD_REQUEST before reaching your flow. Why?
options: The request violated the spec (e.g., wrong query param type)
questions.explanation: The APIkit Router validates requests against the contract before routing.

questions.q: What naming convention binds an APIkit-generated flow to its resource and method?
questions.explanation: APIkit routes by flow-name convention; renaming breaks routing.

questions.q: A subflow differs from a private flow because a subflow:
options: Can have its own error handler
Has neither a source nor its own error handling — it inherits everything from the caller
questions.explanation: Subflows inline into the caller; private flows have their own error handling strategy.

questions.q: Which router executes ALL of its routes concurrently and aggregates the results?
questions.explanation: Scatter-Gather copies the event to every route in parallel and collects route Messages.

questions.q: After a Scatter-Gather, route 1's JSON body is accessed with:
questions.explanation: Aggregation maps index → Message; the body is .payload of that Message.

questions.q: After a For Each completes, the flow's payload is:
questions.explanation: For Each restores the pre-scope payload; use vars (or Parallel For Each) for results.

questions.q: Which condition wins when two Choice 'when' expressions are both true?
questions.explanation: Choice is first-match-wins, evaluated top-down.

questions.q: What does '[1,2,3] map $ * 2' return?
questions.explanation: map applies the lambda to each element; $ is the element.

questions.q: In a reduce lambda '(item, acc) -> ...', what is $$ shorthand for?
questions.explanation: In reduce, $$ is the accumulator — unlike map where $$ is the index.

questions.q: payload groupBy $.city returns:
options: An object keyed by city with arrays of matching records
questions.explanation: groupBy partitions an array into an object of groups.

questions.q: Which expression safely defaults a missing payload.qty to 1?
questions.explanation: default supplies a value when the expression is null/missing.

questions.q: An XML element <order> contains three repeated <item> children. Which selector returns all three?
questions.explanation: The multi-value selector .* collects repeated elements into an array.

questions.q: '2025-12-25' as Date {format: "yyyy-MM-dd"} does what?
options: Formats a date to a string
Parses the string into a Date
questions.explanation: as Date with format PARSES strings; as String with format FORMATS dates.

questions.q: An HTTP Request with default settings receives a 500 from the backend. What happens in the flow?
questions.explanation: Default response validator treats >=400 as error, raising typed HTTP errors.

questions.q: Which Database connector feature prevents SQL injection?
questions.explanation: Parameterized queries are prepared statements — never concatenate user input into SQL.

questions.q: A File listener keeps reprocessing the same file every poll. The standard fix is:
questions.explanation: Without move/delete/watermark, the matcher re-matches the same file forever.

questions.q: Which Salesforce operation inserts or updates in one idempotent call?
questions.explanation: External-ID upsert is the canonical no-duplicates sync operation.

questions.q: An error handled by on-error-continue in an HTTP-sourced flow returns which status by default?
questions.explanation: continue = flow 'succeeded' = success response (200) with the handler's payload.

questions.q: Errors raised inside a subflow are handled by:
questions.explanation: Subflows have no error handlers; errors propagate to the caller.

questions.q: Which expression gives the typed error identifier like NOT_FOUND inside a handler?
questions.explanation: errorType carries namespace and identifier; description is the human text.

questions.q: The correct CloudHub listener configuration is:
questions.explanation: Workers route LB traffic to the app's assigned http.port on all interfaces.

questions.q: Properties for different environments (dev/prod) should be handled by:
options: One artifact + environment-specific property files/deployment properties selected at deploy time
questions.explanation: Build once, externalize configuration — the packaging cardinal rule.

questions.q: A connector dependency in pom.xml must carry which classifier?
questions.explanation: Connectors/modules package as mule-plugin artifacts.

questions.q: Variables set inside a flow called via flow-ref are, after returning to the caller:
questions.explanation: flow-ref shares the event context — variable changes persist both ways.

questions.q: Which statement about ${prop} vs p('prop') is correct?
options: ${} resolves at startup in XML attributes; p() is a DW function evaluated in expressions
questions.explanation: Static placeholder vs runtime DW function — different resolution moments and contexts.

questions.q: Publishing a REST spec to Exchange automatically provides consumers with:
options: An auto-generated connector via REST Connect
questions.explanation: REST Connect turns published specs into Studio-usable connectors.

questions.q: A Try scope's on-error-continue catches an error from one processor. The rest of the flow after the Try:
questions.explanation: continue converts the Try to success; execution proceeds after the scope.

questions.q: Which tool scaffolds Mule flows directly from a RAML/OAS specification?
questions.explanation: APIkit generates the router and per-method flows from the contract.

questions.q: A Mule Domain project is used to:
options: Share resources (HTTP listeners, DB pools, error handlers) among co-deployed apps on the same runtime
questions.explanation: Domain projects eliminate resource duplication when multiple apps share infrastructure on the same Mule server.

questions.q: Which Anypoint component manages worker sizing, app deployments, and environment properties?
questions.explanation: Runtime Manager is the deployment and management control plane for Mule apps across all deployment targets.

questions.q: Which variable scope is destroyed at the end of a flow execution (not shared across async boundaries)?
questions.explanation: Flow variables live for the duration of the processing thread; they cross synchronous flow-refs but not async scopes.

questions.q: The targetValue attribute on an operation is a DataWeave expression evaluated against:
options: The Mule event before the operation
The operation's result (the new Mule event including output payload/attributes)
questions.explanation: targetValue is expressed against the operation's result message, allowing selective extraction before storing.

questions.q: An OAS 3.0 spec uses 'components/schemas' to:
options: Store reusable schema definitions referenced with $ref throughout the spec
questions.explanation: $ref to components/schemas enables DRY type definitions just as RAML's types section does.

questions.q: A RAML overlay differs from an extension in that:
options: An overlay modifies documentation/examples without changing the API contract; an extension can add new traits/resources
Overlays add security; extensions add types
questions.explanation: Overlay = non-breaking doc/example enrichment. Extension = can change the API structure.

questions.q: An Async scope in a flow:
options: Waits for the enclosed processors to complete before continuing
Spins off a new thread; the parent flow continues without waiting for the scope
questions.explanation: Async scope fire-and-forget: parent flow proceeds immediately; variables modified inside don't return to caller.

questions.q: Flow Reference with targetValue stores the called flow's payload into a variable. The calling flow's original payload is:
options: Preserved — targetValue is stored in vars; the original payload is untouched
questions.explanation: target/targetValue pattern on flow-ref stores result in a variable; original event remains unchanged.

questions.q: Which DataWeave function flattens an array of arrays into a single array?
questions.explanation: flatten([[1,2],[3,4]]) → [1,2,3,4]. One level of nesting removed per flatten call.

questions.q: payload filter ($ > 5) applied to [1, 3, 6, 8] returns:
questions.explanation: filter keeps elements where the lambda is true; 6 > 5 and 8 > 5 pass.

questions.q: To transform an array of objects to a CSV string, you output:
questions.explanation: DataWeave's CSV writer serialises array-of-objects to CSV with header row when output type is application/csv.

questions.q: The keysOf() function returns:
options: An array of keys from an object
questions.explanation: keysOf({a:1, b:2}) → ['a','b']. Companion to valuesOf().

questions.q: Lazy evaluation in DataWeave means:
options: Expressions are evaluated only when their value is needed, enabling efficient stream processing
questions.explanation: DW is lazy by design — enables streaming large payloads without loading everything into memory.

questions.q: The HTTP Request connector's 'Response Timeout' property controls:
options: How long the connector waits for a TCP connection
How long it waits for the full response after the connection is established
questions.explanation: Response Timeout is post-connection; Connection Timeout governs the TCP handshake phase.

questions.q: JMS publish-subscribe (topic) vs point-to-point (queue) — with a topic:
options: Only one consumer receives each message
All active subscribers receive each message
Messages are persisted if no subscriber is active by default
Only persistent subscribers get the message
questions.explanation: Topics broadcast; queues compete. Non-durable topic subscribers miss messages published while disconnected.

questions.q: Anypoint MQ's Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) triggers after:
questions.explanation: After maxRedeliveryCount NAck/redeliveries, MQ routes the message to the configured DLQ.

questions.q: Which Salesforce connector operation is used for bulk DML that bypasses most validation rules and triggers?
questions.explanation: Salesforce Bulk API is designed for mass data loads — asynchronous, bypasses real-time limits and many triggers.

questions.q: A flow with an HTTP listener and an on-error-continue that sets payload to 'handled'. The caller receives:
questions.explanation: on-error-continue marks the flow as successful; the listener returns 200 with whatever payload the handler set.

questions.q: A global default error handler applies to a flow when:
options: The flow has its own error handler
The flow has no local error handler defined
questions.explanation: Local handler takes precedence. Global default only activates if no local handler catches the error.

questions.q: Which file defines the Mule app's minimum runtime version and required privileges?
questions.explanation: mule-artifact.json specifies minMuleVersion, classLoaderModel, and other packaging metadata.

questions.q: A Mule 4 app's shared library (JAR) that should be visible to all connectors must be placed in:
options: Referenced in pom.xml — Mule's classloader model resolves it automatically
questions.explanation: Mule 4 uses a plugin classloader model; shared JARs go in pom.xml dependencies, not dropped in directories.

questions.q: Business Groups in Anypoint Platform are used to:
options: Partition organizational units with independent environments, Exchange assets, and access controls
questions.explanation: BGs enable multi-team governance — each BG can have its own environments, Exchange, and user permissions.

questions.q: A Try scope's on-error-propagate re-raises the error. From the perspective of the enclosing flow:
options: The error continues propagating to the flow's error handler
The error is logged and suppressed
questions.explanation: on-error-propagate exits the scope with failure; the error bubbles up to the next handler in the hierarchy.

questions.q: REST Maturity Model (Richardson) Level 3 includes:
options: HATEOAS — responses include hypermedia links guiding clients to next valid actions
questions.explanation: Level 3 (HATEOAS) is the highest RMM level — self-describing APIs where clients navigate via embedded links.
